subject: Product Management
title: Acquisition Metrics
description: A micro-lesson that makes Acquisition Metrics usable.

hook.text: Acquisition answers one question: how do people find your product? If nobody discovers it, nothing else matters.

buildup.text: Key acquisition metrics: traffic by channel, cost per acquisition (CPA), and click-through rate on ads and content.

discovery.text: The best products acquire users cheaply through organic channels — word of mouth, SEO, and viral sharing.

twist.text: High acquisition with low activation is a leaky bucket. Spending more on ads when onboarding is broken just wastes money faster.

climax.text: Smart teams fix activation before scaling acquisition. Fill the holes in the bucket before pouring in more water.

punchline.text: Getting users is easy. Getting them to stay is the real challenge.

quiz.question: Why should teams fix activation before scaling acquisition?
options: Scaling acquisition with broken onboarding wastes money — fix the leaky bucket first
Acquisition is always more important than activation
Activation metrics don't affect business outcomes
